title: Linearized Dispersion Near A Fermi Point
summary: Near an isolated one-dimensional Fermi crossing, the band energy expands linearly as epsilon(p)=epsilon(p0)+v(p-p0)+O((p-p0)^2).
assumptions: The slope at p0 is nonzero.
regime: Small momentum deviation around a one-dimensional crossing.
scope: Lecture One introduction to low-energy effective chiral modes.

source_anchors.notes: This is the starting point for the emergent relativistic form.

representation: Taylor expansion of the energy band around a Fermi point.

symbols.meaning: Fermi velocity given by d epsilon / d p at p0

failure_modes: Quadratic touching requires a different expansion.
retrieval_hints: Use for low-energy continuum approximation near a 1D crossing.
